trade_certifications: Working toward NCCER Core Construction credential

subjects_i_dislike: Higher math (when am I going to use this?)
academics.rigor_self_rating: Lightweight on academic side, intense on shop side
academics.above_and_beyond: I'm at a tech school — half my day is shop. I've been doing electrical trade work for 4 years now. I've already done real wiring jobs as part of school co-op program.
academics.approach_to_school: Academic classes are something to get through. The trade work is where I come alive.

things_built: Wired the entire basement renovation at my uncle's house — passed inspection on the first try
Built a custom workbench with integrated lighting for my dad's garage from scratch
Helped install solar panels on my aunt's roof through a paid summer gig with a local solar company
I rebuild and resell small motors — lawn mowers, weed whackers — on Facebook Marketplace. Made about $1,800 last year.
clubs_leadership: SkillsUSA — competed regionally in Electrical Construction Wiring, placed 4th junior year
Tech school's electrical trade student leadership group — co-leader
work: Summer gig with a local electrical contractor (Stamford-based) — paid, 35 hours/week for two summers. They've already said they'll hire me full-time after graduation.
activities.community_service: Through school's co-op program, helped wire a Habitat for Humanity build. About 60 hours over the summer.

identity.first_gen_college: If I went to college, yes — my parents didn't finish high school in Mexico
identity.religious: Catholic (my family is, I'm chill about it)
identity.other_background: First-gen in the US. My parents work hard and they want me to do well. They're proud I'm at a tech school but they also wonder if I should go to college.

financial_reality.family_situation: We need significant financial aid for anything beyond high school. My parents can't really help with tuition.
financial_reality.comfortable_with_loans: No way — I refuse to go into debt for school
financial_reality.working_during_college: If I go any kind of college, I'd need to work. But I'm leaning toward going straight into the trade.

what_i_want.dream_vibe: Honestly, I want to work. I'm tired of school. I want to make money and build my career as an electrician.
what_i_want.city_vs_campus: N/A — I'm not really thinking campus
what_i_want.distance_from_home: I want to stay close — my family is here, and the contractor that wants to hire me is here
what_i_want.diversity: I'd want to work with people who treat me with respect, regardless of background
what_i_want.instant_no: A path that takes 4 years and puts me in debt before I'm even working
what_i_want.instant_yes: Earning real money within a year while learning a skill that's mine forever

pathway: Apprenticeship (paid training program) — leaning toward IBEW electrical apprenticeship. Open to community college path if it gets me to journeyman faster.

career_direction.today_pick: Licensed electrician. Eventually I want to run my own electrical contracting business.
career_direction.miserable_in_job: Sitting at a desk. Following orders from people who don't know what they're doing.
career_direction.majors_considering: N/A — but if community college, electrical technology certificate or associate's

real_talk.schools_should_know: I've already been working in the trade for 4 years between school and summer jobs. I have a contractor ready to hire me. I'm not figuring out my career — I know what I'm doing.
real_talk.worry: That people will look down on me for not going to college, especially in a town like Stamford. Also that my parents will be disappointed even though they say they support me.
real_talk.success_in_4_years: Journeyman electrician's license in hand, making real money, on track to my own business by my late 20s.
real_talk.anything_else: Don't try to talk me into a 4-year college. Help me find the best apprenticeship program, the right union to join, and any scholarships or stipends for trade school students. That's where I'm headed.
